name: Locust Hills Golf Club
about: Locust Hills Golf Club is a family-owned and operated venue in Springfield, Ohio, founded in 1966. It features two unique 18-hole golf courses, each a par 72, offering a mix of challenges from short doglegs to long open par 5s. The facility provides year-round access through its golf simulator bays, dedicated short game course, and PGA-certified instruction programs. Locust Hills aims to offer a quality golfing experience at an affordable price, catering to a wide range of skill levels with various memberships and event options.
why_golfers_like_it: Year-round access to high-quality Foresight simulators
Affordable pricing with various membership options
Comprehensive practice facilities including a driving range and short game course
PGA-certified instruction available for all skill levels

food_and_drink.notes: Food truck events are held on Fridays
No mention of an alcohol service

simulator_hardware.notes: Two state-of-the-art Foresight GCHawk simulators with access to over 40 virtual courses

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs?
faq.answer: You should bring your own golf clubs to use with the simulators.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.answer: The venue does not have an in-house kitchen or bar, but it hosts food truck events on Fridays.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: Standard simulator time is booked per hour; multi-hour sessions or multiple bookings are required to play a full 18 holes.

faq.question: Is it kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Locust Hills offers junior programs, camps, and a dedicated PGA Junior League, making it suitable for young golfers.
